_meta.name: b3Ɛq Asset Depreciation Rules
_meta.notes: FIRS accepts IFRS-aligned depreciation methods. Useful lives are indicative — adjust per asset condition and management assessment.

categories.label: Motor Vehicles
categories.notes: Cars, vans, trucks — declining balance reflects faster early depreciation

categories.label: Buildings (owned)
categories.notes: Land is NOT depreciated — split land and building on acquisition

categories.label: Leasehold Improvements
categories.notes: Depreciate over shorter of lease term or useful life

categories.label: Right-of-Use Assets (IFRS 16)
categories.notes: Depreciate over lease term. Separate from owned PPE.

categories.label: Software & Licences (Intangible)
categories.notes: Internally developed software may qualify for capitalisation under IAS 38

categories.label: Generator / Power Equipment
categories.notes: Critical Nigerian business asset — typically higher maintenance cost in later years

STRAIGHT_LINE.formula: Annual Depreciation = (Cost - Residual Value) / Useful Life (years)
STRAIGHT_LINE.characteristics: Equal charge each year. Preferred by FIRS for most asset classes.

DECLINING_BALANCE.formula: Annual Depreciation = Net Book Value × (2 / Useful Life)
DECLINING_BALANCE.characteristics: 200% declining balance (double declining). Higher charge in early years. Used for assets with faster economic obsolescence.
